reasoning: The Lean statement encodes "p is an odd prime" as `p.Prime ∧ p > 2`, which is equivalent since 2 is the only even prime. The polynomial expression `∑ n ∈ (f.support \ {0}), f.coeff n * X^(n-1)` with `f = (X+2)^p` correctly computes $\frac{(X+2)^p - 2^p}{X}$: since $(X+2)^p = \sum_{n=0}^p \binom{p}{n} 2^{p-n} X^n$ has all non-zero coefficients (so its support is $\{0,1,\ldots,p\}$), removing index 0 and shifting by $X^{n-1}$ yields exactly $\sum_{n=1}^p \binom{p}{n} 2^{p-n} X^{n-1}$, which equals $((X+2)^p - 2^p)/X$. The irreducibility is claimed in `Polynomial ℤ`, matching $\mathbb{Z}[x]$. Types, hypotheses, and conclusion all match the informal claim.
